payload.answer: Contradiction: one memory says Sam from Goldman has two children, while the new input says Sam from Goldman has no children. Keep both as conflicting claims until clarified.
citations: Existing memory: Sam from Goldman has two children.

payload.decision: Conflict detected; do not overwrite the existing memory. Preserve both claims as mutually inconsistent and temporally ambiguous.

entity_resolution.reason: The two statements refer to the same apparent person description, but they make incompatible claims about children count. Without temporal context or disambiguation, the system should not collapse one into the other.

payload.intent: Explain the contradiction between an existing memory and a new statement about Sam from Goldman’s children.
